Ad Text:
As an AI Technical Lead, you will own and evolve Silverchain's enterprise AI platform and technical delivery of AI to enable safe, cost-effective, and scalable delivery of AI and GenAI solutions across care delivery, corporate, and operational functions. Combine architecture leadership with hands-on enablement to accelerate value, ensure compliance, and uplift team capability.

This is a highly influential role where your ability to connect people, uplift capability, and lead meaningful change will directly impact the future of how Silverchain delivers Best Care in homes and communities nationwide.

What's on offer

Fulltime One-year Fixed Term role. Monday-Friday 76 hours per fortnight.
Hybrid role - working from home, location preferred Perth.
Working for a non-profit organisation, you can salary package up to $18,500 tax free.
Everyday discounts, including discounted vouchers for Coles, Woolworths, Amazon, Myer, The Good Guys and more.
Comprehensive health and wellbeing resources and support.

What we're looking for

To thrive in this role you'll need to have:

Tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence or related fields.
5+ years across software/data/AI and or designing and operating AI/ML platforms at scale.
Strong business writing and presentation skills.
Highly organised, efficient, with exceptional interpersonal skills.
Strong stakeholder engagement and facilitation skills.
Familiarity with Agile ways of working, and the ability to scope, develop, implement, measure and report on innovation activities with agility.
Familiarity with Australian Privacy Act (APPs), clinical governance principles.

Technical:

Cloud (Azure preferred; AWS/GCP ok).
Containers & orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes/AKS)
MLOps/LLMOps (Azure ML/MLflow, model registry, feature store, vector DBs such as Azure AI Search)
CI/CD (GitHub Actions/Azure DevOps), testing strategies, policy-as-code
Observability & model monitoring
GenAI patterns (RAG, prompt engineering, evaluators, guardrails, content filters; agents/orchestration).

The role

As an AI Technical Lead:

Define the end-to-end AI reference architecture (data, models, APIs, RAG, vector search, observability, security, governance).
Select and standardise core platform components (e.g., model hosting, vector DB, feature store, prompt management, experiment tracking, model registry, CI/CD, evaluation harness, guardrails, monitoring).
Establish environment topology (dev/test/stage/prod), multi-tenant patterns, and networking/security baselines.
Support stand-up the platform (cloud resources, clusters, registries, monitoring, cost allocation tags).
Design CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions/Azure DevOps) for reproducible builds for non-prod.
Provide technical leadership and coaching; run design reviews and guilds.
Curate standards and playbooks; champion secure-by-design and privacy-by-default.

Why choose Silverchain

Everyone at Silverchain, from our care teams to leadership, plays a vital role in delivering Best Care. It's a team effort. Best Care is care that is safe, personal, connected and effective.

We are proudly one of the few Australian home care providers accredited in both national health and aged care standards. This means the quality of care we provide is expected to meet the same standards as a hospital or residential aged care facility. As a not-for-profit organisation, we re-invest our funds into research and developing innovative models of care to set the industry benchmark for the provision of the best care in the home.

We'll also provide you with opportunities to help you develop and grow in your career, with training and education programs to help you achieve your goals.

How to apply

To submit your application, click ‘Apply'.

At Silverchain, we recognise the positive contribution that a diverse workforce makes to our organisation where individuals can be authentic, are respected and celebrated. We strongly encourage applications from Indigenous Australians, people of all ages, gender, abilities, culturally diverse and linguistic backgrounds.

3. Discovery & Transparency

structures
language and vocabulary
agreed frameworks and models

This isn’t a traditional BA role, it’s a blend of process analysis, project coordination, and hands on support across several education focused projects. If you’re flexible, collaborative, and prefer to “muck in” rather than stick rigidly to role boundaries, you’ll thrive in this environment.

Job Title: Data Engineering Tech Lead - Databricks, Azure Data Factory, DBT & Python
Ad Text:
Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:




Data Engineering Tech Lead | Data & Analytics | Sydney, AUS

Work on cutting-edge enterprise data solutions using Databricks, Azure Data Factory, DBT, and Python.

Collaborate with a high-performing, agile team to deliver impactful insights across Deloitte.

Continuous professional growth through mentoring, training, and exposure to emerging data engineering technologies.

Deloitte Technology is seeking a skilled Senior Data Engineer to join our Enterprise Data Warehouse (EDW) team in Sydney. This is a permanent role where you'll play a key part in designing, implementing, and optimising scalable data pipelines and workflows that power Deloitte's data-driven initiatives. You'll have the opportunity to work closely with EDW pod leads and the Senior Data Engineering Manager, contributing to high-impact projects across the business.

What will your typical day look like?

Day to day you will:

Develop and maintain scalable data pipelines using Databricks and Azure Data Factory.

Transform raw data into actionable insights using Data Build Tools (DBT) and Python.

Build and maintain data integrations across systems using APIs.

Monitor and optimise workflows for performance and cost-efficiency.

Lead project delivery pods, mentor junior team members, and conduct code reviews.

Collaborate with data modelers, analysts, DBAs, DevOps, and infrastructure teams to deliver high-quality solutions.

Document processes, workflows, and system architecture to maintain clarity and knowledge sharing.

About the team

Deloitte Technology's EDW team powers enterprise-wide data initiatives, enabling business users to make informed, data-driven decisions. You'll join a collaborative and high-performing team working in an agile environment, tackling complex data challenges and continuously improving the EDW platform.

Enough about us, let's talk about you

You may have all or some of the following skills / experiences:

5+ years of experience in data engineering, with expertise in Databricks, SQL, Python, Azure Data Factory, and DBT.

Experience designing, building, and optimising data pipelines and workflows.

Hands-on experience with API-based data integrations and version control systems (Azure DevOps, GitHub).

Strong understanding of data architecture patterns and cloud data storage solutions.

Excellent problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and adaptability.

Collaborative mindset with strong communication and stakeholder management skills.

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field. Advanced degrees or relevant certifications in Azure or Databricks are a plus.

Desirable: Knowledge of Azure Fabric and emerging data engineering technologies.

Why Deloitte?

At Deloitte, we focus our energy on interesting and impactful work. We're always learning, innovating, and setting the standard; making a positive difference to our clients and society. We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - up, into new areas, or across the globe.

We embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, experiences, abilities, and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and cultivate inclusion, making our work environment a space where everyone belongs.

We support flexibility and choice. We encourage you to find the right balance between connecting in person with your clients and teams and meeting your own personal needs.

We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave, and a return-to-work support package.

Next Steps

Sound like the sort of role for you? Apply now, we'd love to hear from you!

What success looks like in this role:

Role Overview

The Regional Transition Management Lead holds full accountability for all transition program across the regional customer portfolio This role ensures that every customer transition in the region—new contracts, renewals, expansions, or service transformations—is delivered with excellence, consistency, and predictable outcomes. The leader drives the maturity of transition practices, develops high performing teams, and embeds enhanced ways of working that scale across all delivery.


Key Responsibilities

Regional Accountability for Transition Delivery

Own the end to end delivery of all transition engagements across the regional customer base, ensuring every program meets scope, quality, budget, and timeline commitments.

Maintain a consolidated view of the regional transition portfolio, proactively managing risks, dependencies, and resource allocation across multiple customers.

Ensure consistent application of transition methodologies, governance standards, and delivery frameworks across enjoyments within regional portfolio.

Act as the senior escalation point for transition related issues, ensuring rapid resolution and stakeholder confidence.

Partner with Sales, Solutioning, and Delivery Leadership to shape transition approaches for new deals and renewals.

Delivery Excellence

Establish strong governance structures for all transitions within regional portfolio, including reporting, risk management, and stakeholder engagement.

Drive operational readiness and ensure seamless handover to steady state service delivery teams.

Monitor performance metrics across the regional transition portfolio and intervene where delivery performance requires improvement.

Ensure compliance with contractual obligations, regulatory requirements, and internal delivery standards across all customer transitions.

Where required directly leads transitions.

Team Leadership & Development

Lead and develop a regional team of Transition Managers, Project Managers, and supporting roles, ensuring capability and capacity align with portfolio demand.

Build a culture of accountability, collaboration, continuous improvement, and delivery excellence across the transition function.

Identify skills gaps and implement targeted development plans to strengthen transition capability across the region.

Promote knowledge sharing, standardization, and best practice adoption across all delivery.

Workload, Chargeability, and Utilization Management

Regional Workload and Capacity Management

Actively manage regional demand against available capacity by aligning work to the appropriate resources based on skills, experience, and role alignment.

Review demand inputs, project pipelines, and operational commitments to identify capacity constraints or excess capacity ahead of delivery impact.

Balance workloads across the region to address under- and over-utilization, ensuring equitable distribution of effort while minimizing delivery risk and employee burnout.

Leverage cross-regional collaboration to address demand spikes, skill shortages, or short-term capacity gaps, ensuring continuity of service and delivery commitments.

Chargeability, Utilization, and Time Entry Oversight

Establish and reinforce clear expectations for chargeability, utilization, and accurate, timely time entry across the regional team.

Monitor utilization, chargeable hours, and non-chargeable activity using standard Unisys reporting and dashboards.

Ensure compliance with Unisys time entry policies, working closely with the team to correct gaps, inconsistencies, or trends impacting reporting accuracy.

Analyze utilization variances versus forecast, identifying root causes such as demand shortfalls, skill mismatches, or delivery inefficiencies.

Forecasting and Financial Alignment

Partner with Finance, PMO, and Delivery leadership to support accurate regional forecasting, including headcount, demand, and revenue projections.

Use Transition and transformation forecasting inputs and historical utilization trends to inform forward-looking resourcing and capacity plans.

Validate forecasts against actuals on a regular cadence and drive corrective actions where gaps are identified.

Support regional and global financial commitments by ensuring utilization performance aligns with revenue and margin expectations.

Productivity and Continuous Improvement

Use productivity, utilization, and time entry data to identify improvement opportunities at both individual and regional levels.

Coach team members on effective time management, prioritization, and chargeability discipline to improve overall performance.

Identify and eliminate non-value-added activities that negatively impact chargeability or delivery efficiency.

Drive continuous improvement initiatives that simplify processes, improve demand visibility, and increase productive utilization.

Performance Reporting and Stakeholder Communication

Provide clear, consistent reporting on workload balance, utilization, chargeability, and forecast accuracy to regional and global stakeholders.

Highlight risks, trends, and exceptions early, along with recommended mitigation or corrective actions.

Use data-driven insights to support workforce planning, hiring decisions, and strategic regional discussions.

Ways of Working & Continuous Improvement

Champion modern, efficient ways of working across all regional transitions, including agile practices, automation, and AI enablement.

Drive standardization of transition methodologies and ensure consistent adoption across the regional portfolio.

Lead retrospectives and lessons learned cycles to embed continuous improvement and organisational learning.

Collaborate with global process owners to refine frameworks, templates, and delivery models based on regional insights.

Influence stakeholders across the region to adopt practices that improve speed, quality, and predictability of transitions.




You will be successful in this role if you have:

Key Skills & Experience

Essential

Proven experience leading multiple large scale IT managed services transitions across multiple customers or regions.

Strong understanding of outsourcing delivery models, service towers, and operational readiness.

Demonstrated ability to manage a complex portfolio of programs simultaneously.

Proven Portfolio Leader with evidencable experience of driving team behaviors

Excellent stakeholder management skills, including C suite engagement.

Strong governance, risk management, and program delivery capabilities.

Exceptional communication, negotiation, and problem-solving skills.

Desirable

Background in managed services across cloud, infrastructure, workplace, and application services.

About AFG

Australian Finance Group (ASX:AFG) is one of Australia's largest mortgage broking groups, founded back in 1994 and now employing 300+ staff across Australia. As a category leader, AFG has some 4,000 finance brokers in its network with access to over 10,000 financial products from more than 80 of Australia's leading lenders. 

Through our broker network, AFG processes around one in 10 of all Australian residential mortgages and manages over $214 billion in residential and commercial finance.

In addition to our broker network, AFG has a rapidly growing home loan business, and an expanding footprint in the provision of business lending solutions.

At AFG our purpose is to create a fairer financial future for all. Our business prides itself its innovative, entrepreneurial and collaborative culture, which is underpinned by our values of integrity, accountability, customer-centric and team player (IACT). Our teams are intricately engaged across the business, and we encourage people with ideas and a willingness to learn to join us.

AFG is committed to our culture. We provide an inclusive workplace where all are welcomed and recognised for their unique ability, regardless of gender, race, religion, disability, orientation, or other identity attribute.

Your new company
Our client is a large, globally operating professional services organisation supporting complex financial and risk-based environments. The business combines strong international capability with a well-established local presence in Australia. Known for its technical expertise and commercial focus, it partners with major clients across multiple industries.

Your new role
This is a senior, commercially focused FP&A role within a large, complex services organisation where finance is central to strategic decision-making. The position partners closely with executive leadership to deliver high quality financial planning, reporting and pricing insight across the Australian business.

Reporting directly to the Chief Financial Officer, the role also provides leadership and guidance to a junior analyst and acts as a key conduit between finance and operational stakeholders.

Key responsibilities include:

Leading monthly financial analysis and management reporting across business units, including forecast and P&L ownership

Delivering commercial insights, recommendations and pricing models to support performance improvement and growth

Supporting contract pricing, RFPs and strategic initiatives with robust financial modelling and analysis

Partnering with senior leaders and executives on budgeting, forecasting, strategy and investment decisions

Driving improvements in reporting, automation, BI utilisation and data integrity across systems

Supporting the CFO with ad hoc analysis, special projects and executive level reporting as required


What you'll need to succeed
This role suits a senior FP&A professional who enjoys operating at both a strategic and hands-on level in a fast-moving, high accountability environment.

You are a commercially minded senior FP&A professional who thrives in complex, multi-stakeholder environments and enjoys partnering with the business to influence outcomes. You bring strong analytical capability balanced with the ability to communicate financial insights clearly to senior leaders.

Ideally, you will bring:
Proven experience in a senior FP&A or financial analyst role, with ownership of planning, forecasting and management reporting

Strong commercial acumen, including pricing models, financial modelling and contract/RFP analysis

Experience partnering with executive and operational leaders, translating data into actionable insight

Advanced Excel capability and confidence working with BI and enterprise financial systems

A proactive, accountable approach with the confidence to work autonomously and manage competing priorities

Tertiary qualifications in finance, accounting or a related discipline, with CPA or CA qualification

This role suits someone who enjoys being close to the business, values high standards, and wants to play a visible role in shaping financial performance and strategy.


What you'll get in return

Senior FP&A role with pricing exposure - own forecasting, reporting and pricing analysis in a commercially focused finance function.

Direct CFO reporting line - high visibility role with influence over strategic planning, budgeting and financial decision-making.

Broad commercial finance remit - combine FP&A, financial modelling, pricing, and performance analysis rather than a narrow reporting role.

Complex, enterprise‑scale environment - work across multiple business units with sophisticated data, systems and stakeholders.

Leadership and progression - mentor junior FP&A talent while building depth in strategic and commercial finance.

Autonomous, trusted position - operate with ownership, accountability and strong executive engagement.


What you need to do now
If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' or forward an up-to-date copy of your CV to jasmine.ee@hays.com.au

If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career.

The Company

This is a business with genuine momentum. A well-established, privately held industrial manufacturing group operating across infrastructure and utilities markets, they have built a strong reputation for delivering critical products to some of Australia's most significant infrastructure projects. The leadership team here is the real drawcard: experienced, invested in their people, and genuinely committed to building careers rather than just filling seats. If you are looking for a platform to grow, this is one worth paying attention to.

The Role

Newly created and sitting within a high-performing commercial finance function, this role has been designed to bring sharper commercial thinking closer to the operational heart of the business. Reporting to the Head of Commercial Finance and based in South Brisbane, you will serve as the primary finance partner to a management team that is engaged, hungry for insight, and ready to act on it.

This is not a reporting role dressed up as something more. You will own the modelling, drive the planning cycles, build the dashboards and sit at the table where decisions actually get made.

Duties will include, but not be limited to:

Acting as the key finance business partner to operational and commercial business units, translating financial data into clear insight for leaders on the ground

Building and maintaining financial models to support planning, pricing, margin analysis and investment decisions

Leading the monthly forecasting process and coordinating the Annual Operating Plan, incorporating the latest business and market conditions

Developing and owning Power BI reporting to track sales, margin and KPIs, ensuring teams stay aligned to targets

Conducting profitability analysis across product lines and identifying opportunities to improve financial performance

Providing financial support for CAPEX submissions, tender preparations, rise and fall calculations and contract management

Monitoring market price movements across key cost drivers including steel, freight and zinc

Partnering with regional finance stakeholders on strategic initiatives, forecasting and planning alignment

Supporting ad hoc analysis and special projects as the business continues to grow and evolve

About You

You are a commercially minded analyst who is equally comfortable building a complex model and walking a non-finance stakeholder through what it means. You have moved beyond simply producing numbers and into shaping how they are used. You thrive in environments where the work is varied, the leaders are accessible and the expectation is that you will bring your own thinking to the table. You are drawn to roles where the function is still being built, because you see that as opportunity rather than ambiguity.

Skills & Experience

To be effective in this role, you will likely bring:

Proven experience in a commercial finance, FP&A or business partnering role, ideally within an industrial, manufacturing or infrastructure environment

Strong financial modelling capability, with the ability to build from scratch and maintain models that drive real decisions

Hands-on experience with Power BI, including dashboard development and data visualisation

Confident business partnering skills, with a track record of building credible relationships with operational and commercial stakeholders

CA or CPA qualification, or progress toward it, will be well regarded

Experience in a listed or complex multi-entity environment will be an advantage, though not essential

What's On Offer

A newly created seat with real scope to shape the role and grow into it

Mentorship and genuine career development within a leadership team that takes that commitment seriously

Exposure across a growing, operationally rich business with infrastructure projects at its core

A competitive remuneration package, aligned to experience

Apply Now

If you are a commercially sharp analyst ready to step into a role with genuine scope and a team that will back your development, apply today.

Click Apply Now to submit your application or contact Jack Gullo at P&C Partners for a confidential discussion.

Our Mission

The NSW Data Analytics Centre's (the DAC) mission is to build world-class capabilities in whole-of-government data analytics to improve outcomes for citizens of NSW. In a world of increasing complexity and rapid change, data analytics has the potential to deliver innovation in service provisions and improve outcomes by generating new perspectives. The NSW DAC was the first of its kind in Australia, and a model now being replicated in other jurisdictions.

The Data Analytics Centre (DAC) exists to lead a whole-of-government approach in tackling some of the State's most difficult policy challenges. Using DAC's Data Analytics Platform, the DAC works to connect data, people, and insights to build world-class capabilities in whole-of-government data analytics to improve health and well-being, safety, social, economic and environmental outcomes for the citizens of NSW.

We encourage you to visit website https://data.nsw.gov.au/nsw-data-analytics-centre to learn more about the DAC.

About the Team

The Data Services team within the NSW Data Analytics Centre (DAC) is a Whole‑of‑Government advanced data and analytics capability, responsible for developing and operating several PSG/CD&I‑owned crown jewels and State Digital Assets. As a network leader across the sector, the team is driving the next evolution of data management, sharing and analytics to accelerate the use of data for good. Our platforms enable secure access to world‑class data engineering, advanced analytics, AI and machine learning, supporting agencies to deliver integrated, high‑impact insights at scale.

Since our founders, the Sisters of Charity, opened our first hospital in 1857, our services and nearly 30,000 people have been behind some of Australia's most important medical breakthroughs. We are a microcosm of Australia's health and aged care system and are uniquely positioned to lead and respond to our rapidly changing environment.

We are looking for a special kind of person - a St Vincent's kind of person. Someone who puts excellence, compassion, connection and caring for other people at the heart of everything they do.

Reporting directly to the General Manager of Procurement, the Senior Procurement Analyst is responsible for providing expert-level design, implementation, and maintenance of procurement analytics across our public, private, and residential aged care markets. You will build the overall capability of analytics within the Group Procurement function, delivering a single source of truth for spend insights, benefits realisation, and optimisation opportunities that drive financial outcomes and continuous improvement across St Vincent's.

This is a Permanent Full-Time position based in any of our Group Head Offices in Sydney, Brisbane, or Melbourne on a hybrid working arrangement

Key responsibilities of the role:

Lead the design and evolution of an enterprise-wide spend cube that serves as the single source of truth for clinical and non-clinical spend across St Vincent's
Utilise advanced analytics and Power BI to automate benefits tracking, identifying cost-saving opportunities and mitigating risks before they impact the business
Partner with Group Procurement to deliver high-impact financial insights that shape departmental budgets and strategic outcomes
Collaborate with Digital & Technology teams to integrate diverse data sources, ensuring our procurement function remains agile and decision-ready
Act as a key technical consultant during major tenders, designing robust quantitative tools and pricing structures that ensure St Vincent's partners with the best in the industry

Things that work for us:

Proven track record of leading procurement or financial analytics within complex, matrixed environments
An expert in Power BI and SQL, with the ability to query large datasets and transform them into modern, automated dashboards
The ability to translate complex numbers into compelling, decision-ready narratives for senior executives
Excel at navigating stakeholder relationships, building consensus, and motivating teams to embrace data-driven cultures
A tertiary qualification in Commerce, Finance, Business, Data Science, or a related discipline

Things that work for you:

Your work directly influences the efficiency of a leading healthcare provider, ensuring more resources go toward exceptional patient and resident care
Enjoy a genuine balance with a hybrid model based out of our modern offices in Sydney, Brisbane, or Melbourne
Competitive salary packaging options
Discounted private health insurance
Employee Assistance Program for staff and their families
Access to the Fitness Passport (FP)

At St Vincent's we value inclusivity and diversity and acknowledge the importance of creating a work environment that is welcoming, safe, fair and impartial and invites people from all backgrounds to join us.

Pre-Employment Checks
Your employment is conditional upon the completion of all required pre-employment checks, including relevant immunisations.

Equal Opportunity
We celebrate diversity & inclusion and are committed to equal employment opportunity for everyone.

How To Apply
Please submit your application via the Apply button, include your resume and a brief cover letter outlining your experience and suitability.

Respectfully, no recruitment agencies.

Job Description:
We are looking for a motivated and technically strong Senior Data Engineer with strong GCP (BigQuery, vertex AI), DBT and SQL experience with 5–15 years of IT experience and a proven track record of working with DBT for the past 1–2 years.

This role involves building and optimizing data pipelines, creating scalable transformation workflows, and enabling reliable data delivery across the organization.

Design, build, and manage scalable data pipelines using GCP services like BigQuery, Cloud Functions, and Cloud Storage

Job Title: General Manager, Enterprise Performance and Insights
Ad Text:
The Organisation

The Greater Metropolitan Cemeteries Trust (GMCT) is seeking a strategic and inclusive senior leader to step into the newly created role of General Manager, Enterprise Performance and Insights.

Reporting to the Chief Governance Officer, this role sits at the centre of GMCT’s enterprise planning, performance and governance ecosystem. You will play a critical role in translating the Trust’s Strategic Plan 2025–2027 into integrated planning frameworks, disciplined project governance, high-quality enterprise performance reporting, and robust corporate information and compliance practices.





The Position

As General Manager, you will provide strategic oversight of GMCT’s enterprise-wide planning and performance frameworks, ensuring strong organisational alignment, transparency and decision-making confidence. You will lead and mature the Enterprise Portfolio Management Office (EPMO), embed consistent project governance practices across directorates, and deliver executive, and Board-level insights that support accountability and long-term sustainability.

The role also oversees corporate information and records management frameworks, ensuring compliance, integrity and access to high-quality organisational information, while fostering a culture of collaboration, inclusion and continuous improvement across the Trust.

 

Key responsibilities

Lead enterprise planning and annual business planning cycles aligned to GMCT’s strategic priorities

Oversee enterprise performance reporting and analytics to strengthen organisational insight and accountability

Embed and continuously improve enterprise project governance and portfolio management practices

Drive continued maturity and innovation within the EPMO

Ensure corporate information and records management frameworks meet governance and compliance requirements

Build strong partnerships across directorates to support a cohesive, high-performing organisation

 

Areas of focus

Data, insights and performance

Data, analytics and organisational insight are core to this role. The General Manager is responsible for: 

Overseeing enterprise performance reporting and analytics, ensuring the Executive and Board receive clear, trusted and decision‑useful insights 

Strengthening the quality, consistency and narrative of performance data across strategy, projects and operations 

Translating complex information into practical insights that drive prioritisation, course‑correction and organisational learning 

Ensuring corporate information, records and data governance frameworks support accuracy, accessibility, compliance and confidence in decision‑making 

 

Stakeholder complexity and Board engagement 

This is a high‑influence, high‑visibility role operating across a complex stakeholder landscape. The General Manager works closely with: 

The Executive Leadership Team on enterprise priorities, performance, risk and delivery 

The Board and Board Committees through reporting, assurance and governance processes 

Senior leaders across directorates to align strategy, performance expectations and delivery discipline 

 

Strategic alignment, culture and organisational impact 

This role has a material impact on how the strategy is experienced and delivered across the organisation. The role: 

Drives alignment between strategic intent and day‑to‑day delivery 

Shapes a culture of accountability, transparency and continuous improvement 

Enables consistent enterprise ways of working across planning, prioritisation and project delivery 

Supports leaders to make informed decisions and lead with clarity and confidence 

 

About you

You are a senior enterprise leader with a strong track record in organisational performance, planning, governance or transformation within complex, regulated or public-facing environments. You bring the credibility to engage at Executive and Board level, paired with a collaborative and inclusive leadership style.

You will demonstrate:

Deep experience in enterprise planning, performance reporting and governance frameworks

Strong program, portfolio or EPMO leadership capability

A practical understanding of information governance, compliance and accountability

The ability to translate strategy into actionable frameworks and measurable outcomes

A values-led, inclusive approach to leadership and stakeholder engagement




For more information on the organisation visit: www.gmct.com.au

Applications are required by: 8th May 2026




To Apply - Please visit https://futureleadership.com.au/opportunities/ click 'Quick apply', using reference GMCepi0426 addressing your cover letter and resume to Peter Barber of Future Leadership™, or call 1300 347 437 for further information.

A candidate information pack is available to download by visiting: https://futureleadership.com.au/your-career/opportunities/

For further information on Future Leadership™ and additional opportunities please visit https://futureleadership.com.au

Business Agility Coach
12-month contract | Competitive day rate | Sydney (Hybrid)
  
I’m working with a leading insurer on a key hire within a large-scale transformation, looking for a Business Agility Coach to help embed outcome-driven delivery across the organisation.

This is a high-impact role working directly with senior leaders and delivery teams, focused on translating strategy into execution using OKRs, Lean and pragmatic agile ways of working.
  
The Role
You’ll be responsible for driving better alignment between business strategy and delivery, ensuring teams are focused on outcomes rather than activity.
Key responsibilities:

Coaching leaders and teams on the adoption and maturity of OKRs
Embedding Lean and Agile ways of working across a complex environment
Facilitating planning, prioritisation and review forums
Supporting better decision-making, trade-offs and focus at leadership level
Partnering with transformation, portfolio and governance teams to align delivery to value
  
About You
You’ll be an experienced coach/operator who has worked in large, complex organisations and can influence at a senior level.
Key experience:

Proven experience coaching OKRs (or similar frameworks)
Strong Agile / Lean delivery background
Experience working with senior stakeholders and leadership teams
Comfortable operating in ambiguity and driving change
Strong facilitation and communication skills
  
The Opportunity

Join a major transformation within a leading insurer
Work at the intersection of strategy and delivery
High-visibility role with genuine impact
Competitive day rate and long-term contract
  
If this sounds of interest, apply now or reach out for a confidential discussion.

Working at Transurban is different; it's a place where you can see the benefits of your work play out in real life, every day. We create city-sized solutions-building and operating safer, smarter, and more sustainable roads-to solve pressing transport challenges that are slowing cities down.





We're seeking a Senior Manager - Customer Transformation to lead a dedicated function of Product Owners and Analysts, championing customer and employee advocacy, and driving scalable Agile ways of working across our Customer and Technology teams. You'll be at the forefront of transforming our business, transformation initiatives, and building roadmaps that align with Transurban's vision and strategy.



You'll join a team focused on transforming, who are collaborative, ambitious and people focused. As our Senior Manager - Customer Transformation, you'll contribute to lasting and positive changes that shape the future of our cities and communities. It's meaningful, challenging and exciting work.





About the role



As our Senior Manager - Customer Transformation, you will be accountable for driving Agile ways of working across existing and new squads as we scale Agile delivery capability across Customer and Technology and setting the standard for our future vision. You will also be accountable for a team working on our Transformation program. This pivotal role will lead teams working through all phases of the delivery lifecycle from idea through to deliver enhanced customer and employee experiences



Day-to-day, you will:








Own and embed our scaled Agile ways of working across Customer and Technology, with a specific focus on the Customer Retail Business. You will lead the implementation of best practice Agile methodologies, driving Agile maturity, managing the flow of work, and facilitating aligned cadences and ceremonies like Quarterly Planning and Squad of Squads across the portfolio.



Lead and grow a high-performing dedicated function of Product Owners and Analysts, providing regular leadership, mentoring, and coaching to all team members. You will ensure appropriate team structures are in place and aligned with the broader Customer & Technology (C&T) functions, as well as the Transformation program.



Be accountable for bringing the C&T Transformation vision to life, across Customer Retail channels and platforms, with clear linkages to C&T Priorities and committed benefits. This includes developing the vision and purpose for both the function.



Establish and maintain effective relationships with key internal and external stakeholders, communicating progress and program health, influencing outcomes, and supporting the Transformation program's objectives. You will also utilise analytics, UX research, and business intelligence tools to evaluate and monitor performance against KPIs, providing insights to inform decisions.







This role will suit someone with a curious mind and transferable skills and experiences, including:






Product management experience working on mass consumer facing products



Agile delivery experience in a complex and dynamic environment



Leadership and management experience



High level business acumen and experience managing budgets and finances



Excellent communication skills, written (including report writing) and verbal and highly advanced influencing and stakeholder management experience



Experience product development software including Jira and Confluence. Solid familiarity with development methodologies, life cycles, and best practices


If you meet some of these requirements, but not all, we encourage you to submit your application.





With a career at Transurban, you'll enjoy a range of benefits, including:






A range of flexible working and leave options, including the option to purchase an additional six weeks of leave each year.



16 weeks paid parental leave (regardless of gender or carer status), with no waiting period, and Superannuation paid during unpaid leave as well.



Learning and development opportunities to support your career interests.



Health and wellbeing support-access to Headspace and our EAP program, wellness facilities, and more.



Share offers and insurance benefits.



Social activities, community give-back programs, and paid volunteer days.





We embrace diversity with a foundation of safety, wellbeing, and inclusion; where everyone belongs. We treat everyone with fairness, consideration, and respect, regardless of gender, gender identity, marital or family status, sexual orientation, age, ethnicity, religious beliefs, cultural or socio-economic background, perspective, and experience. We strongly encourage applications from candidates of diverse backgrounds and lived experiences, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and people living with disability.





Ready to help us keep cities moving? Apply today.

Job Title: Specialist, Global Category Management - 12 month fixed term
Ad Text:
Company description:



About Newmont

Founded in 1921 Newmont Mining Corporation is one of the largest gold mining companies in the world. Newmont prides itself on safety and embraces diversity, engages employees, fosters innovation, rewards high performance and develops great leaders.

Your impact

Reporting to the Director, Global Category Management and embedded within Newmont's Global Supply Chain Operations team in Australia, in the position of Specialist, Camp and Travel you will:

Develop and implement category and sourcing strategies, negotiate and facilitate favorable commercial agreements, and optimize sourcing plans to support global objectives and maximize cost reduction
Manage global supplier relationships, develop and execute Supplier Relationship Management (SRM) programs, and ensure strong working relationships with suppliers for continuous improvement
Serve as the day-to-day interface between business planning, technical services, and other stakeholders to drive contract value, pricing guidance, and achieve budgetary objectives
Maintain awareness of market conditions, lead times, trends, risks, and emerging technologies, focusing on safety, automation, and technological advancements
Proactively lead and coordinate the selection and handling of suppliers across safety, sustainability, and social performance objectives
Apply and manage the utilization of the Request-to-Pay Standard and Category Management frameworks, ensuring compliance, governance, and alignment with stakeholders
Lead the identification and capture of new savings opportunities, manage non-compliance, support Total Cost Ownership (TCO) initiatives, and ensure savings validation and reporting from each initiative
Implement and support continuous improvement opportunities, execute procurement strategies, policies, and guidelines, and enhance procurement capability through training and knowledge sharing
Lead strategic initiatives and portfolio management programs in synchronization with Integrated Business Planning
Support the GCM framework through governance, processes, data requirements and external partnerships.

Prerequisites for your success in the role

Degree qualification in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Accounting, or a related discipline
Deep expertise in strategic sourcing, category and portfolio management, procurement best practices and advanced negotiation strategies
Strong capability in supplier relationship management, including assessment of supplier and contractor financial health and market trends
Demonstrated experience in mining, life of mine environments and global asset intensive industries
Proven ability to manage complex global projects, including developing and managing comprehensive forecasts and budgets
High proficiency in SAP/SAP Ariba, Oniqua and other enterprise level systems
Advanced skills in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and Word
Experience using data analytics and dashboarding tools - i.e. Power BI and Tableau
Full and unrestricted Australian work rights essential.



Job description:



This full-time equivalent position will be offered on a 12-month fixed term basis and will afford the successful candidate the flexibility to work fully remotely from within Australia.

Only those applicants with unrestricted eligibility to work in Australia will be considered.

Job Title: Director - AI & Cloud $400K-$500k base, super, bonus
Ad Text:
Following sustained growth and continued investment in AI and cloud across ANZ, a global consulting leader is expanding its Google Business Group and has created a mandate for a Director – AI & Cloud to drive enterprise transformation at scale.

This is a front-of-market origination role, focused on opening strategic conversations, shaping transformation agendas, and activating complex programs in partnership with Google Cloud.

You will engage directly with executive stakeholders to redefine how organisations operate in an AI-first world, moving beyond technology adoption into true business reinvention.

Why This Role Stands Out
Create, don’t inherit – pure origination focus with autonomy to build strategic pipeline
Strategic alignment with Google Cloud across AI, data and cloud platforms
Operate at C-suite and board level across ANZ enterprise
Work within a fully integrated model – strategy through to execution
Influence high-value, multi-year transformation programs
Direct exposure to ANZ and global leadership
Be part of a growth engine where AI is central to every client conversation
What You’ll Be Responsible For
Originate and shape complex enterprise transformation opportunities
Lead senior client conversations across AI, cloud and operating model change
Frame business-led value propositions, linking technology to measurable outcomes
Build and expand joint opportunities with Google Cloud stakeholders
Qualify and structure early-stage deals, guiding them through to execution

A global leader in enterprise transformation, bringing together strategy, consulting, technology, and operations into a single integrated model designed for the Age of AI.
The Google Business Group is a core strategic pillar, combining deep industry expertise with leading AI and cloud capabilities to help organisations unlock value at scale.

What You Bring

This is a rare opportunity to step into a true market-defining role, shaping how AI and cloud are applied across enterprise organisations in ANZ.

The Role:
Are you passionate about turning data into a real-world impact? Join us as a Senior Data Engineer and help lead a new wave of analytics innovation. You'll be a key part of a dedicated project team delivering high-value, MVP-driven solutions. This role is responsible for advancing our data science practices, from model development to deployment and governance. 
 
What's in it for you? 

Build and deploy statistical models and advanced analytics to solve real business challenges
Influence and uplift our end-to-end data science capability
Work with modern Azure-based platforms
Collaborate with talented teams across analytics, business intelligence, actuarial, data engineering, and business departments
Be part of a values-led organisation with a strong purpose and community focus
Flexible hybrid work arrangement with work from home and in-office
 
Skills & Experience:

For Senior positions - Minimum 7 years' experience as a Data Engineer / Analytics Engineer in a commercial setting.
Bachelor's or higher degree in computer science, information systems, data/analytics engineering, or a related quantitative field.
Proven ability to design, build, and productionise scalable ELT pipelines and analytics datasets (batch and streaming) using modern tooling.
Experience maturing data platforms and engineering practices, including standards for modelling, testing, code review, CI/CD, and governance.
Expertise with Google Cloud data stack—e.g. BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Dataform or dbt (for ELT), Cloud Composer (Airflow), Dataflow/Beam, Pub/Sub, Cloud Run—and strong familiarity with Looker semantic modelling (LookML).

Experience in Insurance or Banking 
Power BI skills 
Microsoft Azure Certifications

Established since 1953 offering stability and security, a great place to grow and develop your career. Due to continuous growth, we have a new opportunity within our Capital Markets, Australia department. This role will support the origination, structuring, pricing, execution, and distribution of syndicated loan transactions across both underwritten and non‑underwritten deals. The position works closely with stakeholders across Corporate Banking, Structured | Project Finance and other front office department; and plays an active role in supporting senior team members in developing and maintaining strong relationships across SMBC, as well as with bank and non‑bank investors in the Australian and Asia Pacific markets.

Reporting to the Executive Director, this position will be responsible for:

Assist with origination, structuring, pricing, underwriting, execution, bookrunning, sell‑down, and distribution of syndicated transactions.
Prepare credit analysis, liquidity analysis, sell‑down strategies, underwriting papers, term sheets, offer letters, financing proposals, and information memoranda.
Support internal and external reporting, marketing materials, publicity materials, and management‑requested research (including loan market data analysis etc).
Coordinate syndication processes including bank meetings, presentations, secondary loan trading, documentation and due diligence reviews, and liaison with legal counsel and third‑party advisors.
Contribute to ESG‑related workstreams, including pitching, execution, reporting, and external engagement.
Collaborate with Corporate Banking and Structured Finance teams to support client coverage and generate MLAB syndication opportunities.
Work closely with regional and global teams to identify new investor opportunities and refer debt transactions appropriately.
Uphold and enhance SMBC's reputation through timely, efficient execution and a strong "Team SMBC / Team Sydney" approach.
Preparing underwriting papers in conjunction with senior managers to present to Capital Markets Group, SFDA and CDAPD.

To succeed in this role, you will have:

3-5 years of banking experience in corporate banking and/or a syndication role, preferably within a major Australian bank.
Experience in loan structuring and negotiating terms and conditions with clients, credit teams, and legal counsel.
Established relationships with clients and banking counterparts in the Australian market (preferred but not a requirement).
Well‑developed interpersonal and influencing capabilities, with experience managing key internal and external stakeholders.
Strong time management and organisational skills, with the ability to manage competing priorities.
Sound sales and negotiation skills, or a demonstrated willingness to develop these through on‑the‑job experience and training.
Ability to deliver high‑quality presentations to senior internal stakeholders and external clients.
Strong written and verbal business communication skills.
High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
University qualification in Banking, Finance, or a related discipline.

This organisation is a publicly owned utilities provider delivering critical services to the Canberra community and surrounding region.
 
They are currently undergoing a significant digital and data transformation, positioning data as a key enabler of future service delivery
 
The Opportunity

As part of this transformation, Leadership & People Enablement has been identified as a strategic priority, with workforce planning playing a key role in enabling this.

This is a greenfield initiative, with no existing workforce planning capability in place. An external consulting agency has recently delivered the strategy and frameworks the focus now is on building the capability and bringing it to life.

To support this, the team is bringing in a data-led Power BI Analyst to shape how workforce insights are delivered across the business.

You’ll be supported by a highly experienced, positive and engaging manager with deep SME knowledge in workforce planning who will you give you the business context while you focus on the data. 
 
The Role

This is a hands-on data role focused on building Power BI dashboards and shaping workforce insights for this. You’ll work across multiple data sources from Aurion (including legacy) & ELMO to build dashboards and reporting that inform workforce decisions at an operational and executive level.

Key responsibilities include:

Build dashboards and reports in Power BI to support workforce planning
Extract, clean and wrangle data from multiple systems using Excel & Power Query
Translate data into clear insights to support business decision-making
The Ideal Person

This role will suit an experienced Data Analyst (3+ years) who has worked with workforce/HR data or is interested in learning this space rather than a workforce planner who uses dashboards.

You’ll bring experience in:

Power BI (intermediate or above), including DAX
Cleaning and wrangling data using Excel and Power Query
Presenting insights to a wide range of non-technical stakeholders
Workforce/HR data (or a strong interest in learning)
Salary / Rate

Full-time (36.05 hours/week) labour hire contract through to February 2027
Estimated rate: $90/hr – $105/hr + 12% Super
How to Apply

To apply online, please click on the appropriate link.
 
Alternatively, for a confidential discussion, please contact Lucas Walker on 0483 911 561 quoting ref no. 21527
 
HorizonOne is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce and we encourage applications from people of all cultures, capabilities and backgrounds.

Overview

Alchemy Partners is an Australian-owned and led boutique consulting firm that helps organisations improve business performance through better collaboration.

We partner with senior leaders across public and private sectors to design and deliver solutions to complex, systemic challenges. Our work spans strategy, transformation, operating models, technology advisory, program and project delivery, major event execution, data insights and AI, change management and strategic communications.

Collaboration sits at the heart of how we work - bringing together diverse perspectives to deliver practical, high-impact outcomes.




The Opportunity

We are seeking a Manager – Co-Design & Human-Centred Solutions to join our Canberra team.

This role sits at the intersection of human-centred design, co-design and consulting delivery - working with clients to deeply understand problems, engage stakeholders and co-create solutions that are practical, implementable and impactful.

You will initially work within a Collaboration Hub environment, partnering with clients and stakeholders to tackle complex challenges in real time - bringing structure, clarity and momentum to ambiguous problems.

This is a hands-on, delivery-focused role suited to someone who enjoys working closely with people, navigating complexity, and shaping solutions through collaboration.




What You’ll Do

Co-Design & Problem Solving

Work with clients to frame complex problems, identify root causes and define clear outcomes

Apply human-centred design approaches to understand user needs, behaviours and pain points

Lead and support co-design activities that bring together diverse stakeholders to shape solutions

Translate insights into practical recommendations, concepts and delivery pathways

Collaboration & Engagement

Design and deliver collaborative engagements that drive alignment and shared ownership

Facilitate workshops, working sessions and stakeholder forums where appropriate

Build strong relationships with client stakeholders, acting as a trusted delivery partner

Operate effectively in multi-disciplinary environments, working alongside operations, technology and delivery teams

Delivery & Consulting

Manage workstreams and contribute to broader client engagements

Ensure outputs are clear, structured and aligned to client needs

Support the progression of ideas from concept through to implementation

Contribute to high-quality client outcomes across a range of sectors

Leadership & Contribution

Contribute to the evolution of Alchemy’s co-design, facilitation and collaboration methodologies

Build internal capability in human-centred design and collaborative problem solving

Strengthen client relationships and contribute to repeat and referral work




About You

You are a pragmatic and people-focused consultant who enjoys working through complexity with others and turning ideas into action.

You will bring:

Incolink provides a safety net for workers in the commercial building and construction industry where permanency and continuity of employment are significant issues. Supp is a fast-growing jobs platform connecting over 200,000 hospitality professionals with more than 8,000 independent venues across Australia and the US. As we continue to grow, we're looking for talented people to join us and help shape the future of hospitality.

We're looking for an Analytics Engineer to work within our ML and Analytics team - someone skilled at helping others extract meaningful insight from data and make a real impact on the future of our company.

You'll also help drive our Data Platform, ensuring that our data is secure, high quality, and ready to be accessed efficiently to guide business decisions.

We are not looking for an Excel specialist, but someone with experience with fully-fledged Data Platforms and is comfortable with BI systems such as Data Studio, or Tableau.

About the platform

Supp is a dynamic two-sided marketplace connecting businesses and hospitality professionals in real time.

We use data-driven matching to pair the right candidates with the right opportunities.

Size

Teamwork

Empowerment

Location

Part-time friendly

Successful candidates
